title: Sainte-Chapelle - Art and Architecture of the Middle Ages
highlights: 1248  ...    Description: The Sainte-Chapelle (Holy Chapel) in Paris was commissioned by King Louis IX (r. 1226–70) to house relics of the Passion that he purchased in 1239 from his cousin, the king of Constantinople  ...    relics, Louis hoped to make Paris a new Jerusalem. The Sainte-Chapelle was a monumental reliquary attached to the royal palace; its lower level served as the parish church for the palace staff. The upper chapel is remarkable for the abundance of stained glass, over 1100 glass panels in tall lancet windows; the walls have almost disappeared. Thin walls, spiky pinnacles, steep gables, and radiating tracery are components of the rayonnant style widely used in Paris at that time. The lower zones were richly embellished with polychromy, enamel, and sculptures of the apostles. When the relics  ...    Christ wearing the crown in the central Passion window.
